architecture.summary: DLM-Scope applies Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) to diffusion language models (DLMs) to extract and intervene on sparse, interpretable features at hidden layer activations. The system trains Top-K SAEs that decompose DLM activations into interpretable basis vectors, enabling feature extraction and steering interventions across diffusion timesteps. Information flows from frozen DLM hidden states into trainable SAE encoders/decoders, with reconstructed activations optionally reinjected into the model.

biblio.title: DLM-Scope: Mechanistic Interpretability of Diffusion Language Models via Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: Trained Top-K SAEs can faithfully extract interpretable features from DLMs
SAE insertion in DLMs can reduce cross-entropy loss when applied to early layers, a phenomenon absent or markedly weaker in autoregressive LLMs
SAE features in DLMs enable more effective diffusion-time interventions, often outperforming LLM steering
SAEs can provide useful signals for DLM decoding order
SAE features are stable during the post-training phase of DLMs
